QF.id: XIII Introduction to Forwards and Futures_59

question.text: What is the maximum position limit for a client in USDINR futures across all exchanges?

answer.text: USD 20 million net long or short across all exchanges combined
There is no position limit for USDINR futures

explanation.text: **The Logic:** For USDINR futures, the client-level position limit is USD 20 million (net long or net short) across all exchanges. This is lower than the trading member limit (higher of Rs 500 crore or 15% of total OI) and the bank trading member limit (higher of Rs 1000 crore or 15% of total OI). **The Trap:** USD 100 million is the bank TM limit. USD 5 million is too low. Position limits absolutely exist for currency derivatives. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series I Ch7 specifies USDINR position limits. Compare with equity MWPL (20% of free float) in Series VIII. Position limit structures differ across markets. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The USD 20M client limit means large hedgers may need to access the OTC forward market for exposures beyond this — the exchange-traded market has capacity constraints for very large positions.
